chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي وَحْدَهُ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُ فِي الْجَمَاعَةِ أَوْ يُصَلِّي بِقَوْمٍ وَ قَدْ كَانَ صَلَّى قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 55 – The Salāt alone, then he repeats in the Jam’at, or he prays Salāt with a group and he had already prayed before that

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/268)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/268)
text: الرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي الصَّلَاةَ وَحْدَهُ ثُمَّ يَجِدُ جَمَاعَةً قَالَ يُصَلِّي مَعَهُمْ وَ يَجْعَلُهَا الْفَرِيضَةَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, and Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, altogether from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hafs Bin Al Bakhtary,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding the man who prays the Salāt alone, then he finds a Jam’at. Heasws said: ‘He should pray Salāt with them, and he should make it to be an Oblligatory (Salāt)’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn ’Isma’il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan and Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from all from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hafs ibn al-Bakhtariy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ’Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who performs Salah (prayer) alone then finds a congregation. He (the Imam) said, ’He can perform Salah (prayer) with them and make it to be an obligatory one.”’
